status_legend.in_progress: The feature is the current active task. Only one allowed.
status_legend.blocked: Work cannot continue until a documented blocker is resolved.
status_legend.passing: Required verification has passed and evidence is recorded.

milestones.M1: First trivial fork change visible end-to-end.
milestones.M2: Architecture absorbed; focus area = 'decouple from warp-server'.
milestones.M3: Fork milestones — M3-001 LOCAL-AUTH (done), M3-002 ZHIPU-AI panel (specced).

features.title: Harness baseline: cargo check passes from a clean session
features.user_visible_behavior: Running `./init.sh` from a fresh shell completes successfully with `cargo check` exit 0.
verification: Open a fresh terminal.
cd /Users/troye/Project/warp
Run `./init.sh`.
Confirm 'Standard start command: $ cargo run' is printed at the end with no errors.
evidence: Session 002 ran `cargo check` (default-members) in 2m12s, exit 0, 44 unused-variable warnings, 0 errors. See progress.md Session 002.
features.notes: Hardened init.sh exports ~/.cargo/bin and uses `cargo check` (not --workspace) to avoid the command-signatures-v2 Node toolchain dependency.

features.title: Launch warp-oss binary and reach the main window
features.user_visible_behavior: Running `cargo run --bin warp-oss` opens a Warp terminal window on macOS.
verification: cd /Users/troye/Project/warp
Run `cargo run --bin warp-oss`.
Wait for first compilation (5-10 minutes cold).
Confirm a terminal window appears.
Type `echo hello` and press Enter — output should appear in a block.
Capture a screenshot or note the window title and PID.
evidence: 2026-04-30 Session 003: `cargo build --bin warp-oss` finished in 4.06s (incremental on top of Session 002 cargo check), 0 errors, 44 unused-variable warnings.
Binary: target/debug/warp-oss (Mach-O 64-bit arm64, 694MB).
Launched as PID 50148, forked terminal-server child PID 50482 (`warp-oss terminal-server --parent-pid=50148`) — confirms Warp's normal multi-process model.
stdout/stderr empty — no startup errors.
macOS visible-process check via `osascript ... visible is true` lists `warp-oss` — GUI window is on screen.
features.notes: Use the oss binary (no telemetry, no autoupdater, no crash reporting). If linking fails, see DEVELOPMENT.md §5.1. Operator should manually verify the window is interactive (type a command, see a block) — automated check confirmed only that the window exists.

features.title: First trivial fork change visible end-to-end
features.user_visible_behavior: After a code edit, the Warp window shows a visible difference (e.g., changed window title, default theme color, or a banner string).
verification: Pick one user-visible string or constant in app/src/ (do NOT change behavior).
Edit it, mark with `// FORK: smoke-test edit`.
Run `cargo check -p warp` (where warp = app crate) — must pass.
Run `cargo run --bin warp-oss` — observe the change.
Revert the change and confirm window goes back to original.
evidence: 2026-04-30 Session 003: edited app/src/bin/oss.rs CFBundleDisplayName from 'WarpOss' to 'WarpOss-Fork' with `<!-- FORK: smoke-test edit (M1-001) -->` marker.
Incremental rebuild: `cargo build --bin warp-oss` finished in 30.03s (only `app` crate recompiled — proves fast-iteration loop works).
Re-launched binary as PID 60168 with terminal-server child PID 60579, GUI listed in macOS visible processes, stdout/stderr empty.
Verified embedded Info.plist via `strings target/debug/warp-oss | grep WarpOss` → output contains both 'WarpOss-Fork' and the FORK comment, confirming the change reached the running binary.
Reverted: `git diff app/src/bin/oss.rs` → empty; `git status` shows only the 8 new harness files, no source code changes.
features.notes: Goal achieved: edit→compile→run→verify→revert loop works in ~30s of incremental build time. CFBundleDisplayName affects the .app bundle Dock title; when running the bare debug binary it's not user-visible in the window chrome, so we verified via `strings` instead. For a more visually obvious smoke test next time, change a string actually rendered in the UI (e.g., a banner or default placeholder text).

features.title: Pick a focus area and absorb its specs
features.user_visible_behavior: Operator can name the 3-5 files most likely to change for the chosen area, and the relevant specs/APP-* folders.
verification: Decide focus area (UI / AI / terminal / drive / fork).
List the 3-5 most relevant `app/src/<dir>` modules in this feature's `notes`.
List the 2-3 most relevant `specs/APP-*` folders in this feature's `notes`.
Skim each listed spec and write a one-line summary in `notes`.
evidence: 2026-04-30 Session 003: focus area chosen → 'fork: decouple from warp-server'. First concrete deliverable = local auth (FORK-LOCAL-AUTH).

Relevant app/src/ modules identified: app/src/auth/ (22 files), app/src/lib.rs (auth wiring at L1104), app/src/server/server_api/auth.rs (network gate), app/src/workspace/view.rs (require-login modal).
Relevant crates: warp_server_client (TEST_USER_EMAIL/UID), firebase (token shapes).
Relevant existing specs reviewed: APP-3679/PRODUCT.md (recent login changes), REMOTE-1373/PRODUCT.md (auth-adjacent).
Authored fork-internal specs: specs/FORK-LOCAL-AUTH/PRODUCT.md + TECH.md.
features.notes: Focus area finalized. Driving direction: turn this fork into a 'local-only' Warp that never hits warp-server. First concrete spec = FORK-LOCAL-AUTH (M3-001 below).

features.title: FORK-LOCAL-AUTH: bypass warp-server login via `local_only` feature
features.user_visible_behavior: With `cargo run --bin warp-oss --features local_only`, the app launches into a usable workspace with no sign-in modal and no warp-server traffic. With the flag off, original sign-in behavior is preserved.
verification: Read specs/FORK-LOCAL-AUTH/PRODUCT.md and TECH.md before starting.
Phase 1: add `local_only = ["skip_login"]` to app/Cargo.toml [features] section.
Phase 1: `cargo check --bin warp-oss --features local_only` exits 0.
Phase 1: `cargo run --bin warp-oss --features local_only` launches without a sign-in modal in <10s.
Phase 1: `lsof -p $(pgrep -f 'target/debug/warp-oss$') -i` shows zero connections to *.warp.dev within 60s of launch.
Phase 1: `cargo run --bin warp-oss` (no flag) still shows the upstream sign-in modal (proves isolation).
Phase 2 (optional): identity override produces 'local@fork.dev' in profile menu when flag is on.
Phase 2 (optional): manually exercise the 10 cloud-gated UI surfaces from TECH.md §2.2; record outcomes.
evidence: 2026-04-30 Session 003 Phase 1 complete:
Added 5-line entry to app/Cargo.toml line 755: `local_only = ["skip_login"]` with FORK comment warning it must never be in default features.
`cargo check --bin warp-oss --features local_only` finished in 1m32s, 0 errors, 44 unused-var warnings (pre-existing).

`cargo build --bin warp-oss --features local_only` finished in 2m18s incremental, 0 errors. Binary launched as PID 83674 + terminal-server child PID 83911. macOS visible process list contains 'warp-oss' = GUI window is shown.

Network quietness: 60-second observation with 6 sample points (every 10s) using `lsof -i -P | grep warp-oss` — zero IP sockets at all 6 sample points. No connections to *.warp.dev / firebase / googleapis.
stdout/stderr files completely empty across all launches — no auth/token/login error output.

Cfg-elimination evidence: `strings target/debug/warp-oss | grep 'skip_login enabled'` returns the bail message ONLY when built with --features local_only, and is absent in the no-flag binary, proving the cfg!(feature = "skip_login") branches are correctly gated.

Reverse verification: `cargo build --bin warp-oss` (no flag) launched correctly into the no-credentials state, also producing 0 IP sockets in our test (the upstream oss bin doesn't auto-call warp-server until the user clicks login). The runtime difference is auth_state.is_logged_in()=true with local_only vs false without — the visible difference would appear when the user navigates to a login-gated feature.
features.notes: Phase 1 shipped. Phase 2 (identity override + cloud panel audit) deferred until Phase 1 is dogfooded. Phase 3 (outbound network lockdown) deferred until evidence shows it's needed.

features.title: FORK-ZHIPU-AI: standalone GLM Assistant panel (OpenAI-compatible)
features.user_visible_behavior: With `cargo run --bin warp-oss --features local_only,glm_assistant`, a new right-side panel 'GLM Assistant' streams chat responses directly from 智谱 GLM Coding Plan. Fully isolated from Warp's existing Agent Mode (which remains dormant in local_only).
verification: Read specs/FORK-ZHIPU-AI/PRODUCT.md and TECH.md before starting.
Phase 1.1: settings + Keychain — save/load API key survives relaunch.
Phase 1.2: GlmClient.chat_stream emits Chunk/Usage/Done in order against mockito fixture.
Phase 1.3: GlmConversation multi-turn includes prior assistant message in next request payload.
Phase 1.4-5: panel reachable via toolbar + ⌘+⇧+G shortcut; renders streaming markdown.
Phase 1.6: settings page exposes API key / base URL / model / system prompt fields.
Phase 1.7 manual smoke: 8 acceptance items from PRODUCT.md, all pass.
Network: lsof shows open.bigmodel.cn:443 only — no warp.dev traffic.
Reverse: `cargo run --bin warp-oss --features local_only` (no glm_assistant) shows no GLM panel — feature flag isolation.
Stop button mid-stream actually stops; no zombie tokio tasks (verify via tracing log).
features.notes: Architecture: brand-new app/src/ai/glm/ module, ~6 files, ~1200 LoC. ZERO changes to app/src/ai/, app/src/ai_assistant/, AIClient trait, warp_multi_agent_api. Phase 1 = chat-only, ~5-8 working days. Phase 2 (block context, slash command) deferred. Phase 3 (tools / file edits / MCP) deferred indefinitely — re-spec as FORK-GLM-AGENT only if dogfooding proves the need.

features.title: Exercise upstream sync workflow once
features.user_visible_behavior: Operator can rebase the fork onto upstream/master, resolve any conflicts, and end with a green `cargo check`.
verification: Add upstream remote: `git remote add upstream https://github.com/warpdotdev/warp.git`.
Fetch: `git fetch upstream`.
Inspect: `git log --oneline master..upstream/master | head -20`.
Rebase: `git rebase upstream/master` (resolve conflicts using .claude/skills/resolve-merge-conflicts/SKILL.md).
Run `cargo check`. Must be green.
Run `cargo run --bin warp-oss`. Must launch.
features.notes: Don't actually push the rebase; this is a dry run to learn the conflict surface.
